etymology.description: semantic 邑 (yì, "county; town") + phonetic 良 (liáng) -> young man
etymology.interpretation: The character 郎 (láng, "young man") combines the phonetic component 良 (liáng, "good") with the semantic component 邑 (yì, "county; town"). The phonetic component 良 suggests the pronunciation of the character, while the semantic component 邑 indicates the historical setting or origin from a town or county, reflecting the role of young men as civil servants or those belonging to a locality.

examples.chinese: 那个年轻的郎吸引了很多人的注意(那個年輕的郎吸引了很多人的注意)。
examples.pinyin: Nàgè niánqīng de láng xīyǐnle hěnduō rén de zhùyì.
examples.english: That young man attracted a lot of attention.

examples.chinese: 她嫁给了一个勤劳的郎(她嫁給了一個勤勞的郎)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jià gěi le yī gè qínláo de láng.
examples.english: She married a hardworking young man.

examples.chinese: 庄里来了一个新的郎(莊裡來了一個新的郎)，大家都很好奇(大家都很好奇)。
examples.pinyin: Zhuāng lǐ lái le yī gè xīn de láng，dàjiā dōu hěn hàoqí.
examples.english: A new young man came to the village, and everyone was curious.
